question: Is the [Japanese anime-style ballerina] positioned leaping high in the air, suspended above the [crouching male dancer]?
Is the [watercolor-rendered dancer] positioned in the midground and partially obscured by foreground figures?
Is the leaping ballerina rendered in a [Japanese anime] style (e.g., cel-shading, large eyes, clean outlines)?
Is the crouching male dancer rendered in a [Cubist] style (e.g., geometric fragmentation, multi-perspective planes, angular decomposition)?
Is the spinning female dancer rendered in an [Art Deco] style (e.g., streamlined geometric forms, decorative elegance, extended arms)?
Is the midground dancer rendered in a [watercolor] style (e.g., translucent washes, soft edges, fluid blending)?
Do the spotlights cast light composed of distinct colored dots, characteristic of [Pointillism]?
Is the background stage curtain rendered in an [Expressionist] style with prominent red and black swirling forms?
Are the distinct art styles (Anime, Cubist, Art Deco, Watercolor) applied specifically to their respective characters without blending into a uniform style across the image?
